title: autoresearch: Autonomous Research Loop with a Local LLM Critic (v1.0)
description: Generalization of the karpathy/autoresearch pattern as a small Python package: any optimization problem expressible as a single metric, a fixed time budget, a single mutable file and a git branch can be optimized iteratively by an LLM critic that proposes one structured change at a time. The critic runs locally via Ollama (any OpenAI-compatible endpoint also works) and emits JSON-Schema-constrained proposals (thought_process, alternatives_considered, hypothesis, expected_delta, justification, code_pseudocode, risk_level). The harness commits, runs the experiment under a hard timeout, classifies the result as keep/discard/crash against the running best, advances or resets the git branch, and consolidates a navigable audit log. Ships three working examples (TSP heuristic search, XGBoost hyperparameter tuning, multi-metric scalarization) with sample runs.

creators.name: Brom, Pedro Carvalho
creators.affiliation: Department of Mathematics, Federal Institute of Brasilia
